channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: European leaders want a say in talks to end Russia's war in Ukraine. The US is brushing them off
item.description: PARIS (AP) — European leaders insisted Monday they must have a say in international talks to end the war in Ukraine despite the clear message from both Washington and Moscow that there was no role for them as yet in negotiations that could shape the future of the continent.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and RAF CASERT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A look at recent aviation disasters in North America
item.description: A Delta Airlines plane arriving Monday at Toronto’s Pearson Airport from Minneapolis flipped and landed on its roof, injuring at least 17 people.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Tongolele, iconic dancer of the golden era of Mexican cinema, dies at 93
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Tongolele, the iconic American dancer who transformed the nightlife scene in mid-20th century Mexico City with her hip movements to the sound of drums, has died at the age of 93. Mexico's secretary of culture announced her death Monday on X.
item.dc:creator: BERENICE BAUTISTA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Gov. Ron DeSantis' former chief of staff James Uthmeier sworn in as Florida’s attorney general
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis on Monday named his former chief of staff, James Uthmeier as Florida’s attorney general, handing the state’s top law enforcement role to a trusted aide who has helped orchestrate and defend some of his most divisive initiatives.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT and KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What is a polar vortex? US gets a taste of the Arctic this week
item.description: The term “polar vortex” usually refers to the gigantic circular upper air weather pattern in the Arctic region. It is a normal pattern that is stronger in the winter and keeps some of the coldest weather bottled up near the North Pole.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump suggests no laws violated if he ‘saves’ country
item.description: Trump doubled down on the Napoleon reference on Sunday, reposting an image of the French emperor riding into battle with the text "He who saves his country violates no law" overlaid.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: SpaceX employees visiting FAA amid reported firings
item.description: Members of the SpaceX team will "envision how we can make a new, better, modern and safer system," Sean Duffy said.
item.dc:creator: Steven Joachim, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mexican singer Paquita la del Barrio dies at 77
item.description: Legendary Mexican singer Paquita la del Barrio has passed away at 77 years old. Paquita, whose real name is Francisca Viveros Barradas, passed away at her home in Veracruz, Mexico. Her family revealed her passing in a post on Monday morning on Instagram.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KRON4

item.title: Eagles player declines actor’s offer to pay Super Bowl fine
item.description: Actor and Eagles fan Rob McElhenny, best known for co-creating and starring in FXX’s “It’s Always Sunny in Philadelphia,” had recently offered to foot the bill for a fine incurred by Eagles player Jalyx Hunt during Super Bowl LIX.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Trump suggests no laws violated if he ‘saves’ country
item.description: Trump doubled down on the Napoleon reference on Sunday, reposting an image of the French emperor riding into battle with the text "He who saves his country violates no law" overlaid.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Illegal-smuggling coyotes now advertising at Canada border amid Trump migrant crackdown: report
item.description: Canada-based human smugglers are allegedly exploiting TikTok to advertise illegal entry into the US across the northern border amid Trump's southern crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean actress Kim Sae-ron's cause of death revealed
item.description: The cause of death of South Korean actress Kim Sae-ron has been revealed. The 24-year-old's death has been ruled a suicide by the Seongdong Police Station in Seoul, according to The Korea Times.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: FAA firings begin weeks after deadly crash near DC
item.description: The Trump administration has begun firing hundreds of employees at the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) just weeks after a deadly crash outside of Washington, D.C., put a spotlight on the agency.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Five pressing questions as RFK Jr. takes HHS reins
item.description: His entire Make America Healthy Again movement is based largely on undermining the scientific and public health messaging from the federal government, along with overhauling the status quo in the food and drug industries.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KRON4

item.title: Washington Post backs out of 'Fire Elon Musk' ad order
item.description: The Washington Post this week backed out of a “Fire Elon Musk” advertising order that was to run as a wrap on some of its Tuesday editions, according to the advocacy group Common Cause.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: William Byron avoids late wrecks to win 2nd straight Daytona 500
item.description: Ninth in the No. 24 Chevrolet with one lap left, Byron was in the right place by racing near the outside wall in overtime to became the first back-to-back winner since Denny Hamlin in 2019-20.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean actress Kim Sae-ron found dead: reports
item.description: Kim Sae-ron, who appeared in Netflix's "Bloodhounds," among other projects, was found dead at her home on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, ABC 4

item.title: Transgender man found dead in New York field endured repeated acts torture: police
item.description: “This is one of the most horrific crimes I've ever investigated,” Captain Kelly Swift of the New York State Police Bureau of Criminal Investigation said.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, ABC 4

item.title: Scientists are racing to discover the depth of ocean damage sparked by the LA wildfires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — On a recent Sunday, Tracy Quinn drove down the Pacific Coast Highway to assess damage wrought upon the coastline by the Palisades Fire. The water line was darkened by ash. Burnt remnants of washing machines and dryers and metal appliances were strewn about the shoreline.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Missouri community supports local Mexican restaurant after employees detained
item.description: One week after federal agents made an unannounced visit to El Potro Mexican Café in Liberty, their community came to help the restaurant on Valentine’s Day.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Sloan, KTLA 5

item.title: Investigators enter a highway tunnel in Wyoming where a fiery crash killed 2
item.description: GREEN RIVER, Wyo. (AP) — Investigators in Wyoming began sifting through the wreckage inside a highway tunnel Saturday after smoke, hazardous chemicals and structural concerns prevented them from determining how many people may have died in the fiery pileup.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Here are the agencies purging federal workers
item.description: The moves come as President Trump's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), led by tech billionaire Elon Musk, have exacerbated their efforts to cut down on government spending — in some cases looking to dismantle whole agencies or departments.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Sheryl Crow sells Tesla to donate funds to embattled NPR
item.description: “Money donated to NPR, which is under threat by President Musk, in hopes that the truth will continue to find its way to those willing to know the truth,” the country artist wrote.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The new NBA All-Star format is here, and Sunday night will show whether it works
item.description: This will be an actual All-Star matchup. On one side, there's a team with 86 combined All-Star selections, $2.7 billion in on-court earnings and where every single player on the team is either an NBA champion, an Olympic gold medalist, or both.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: IRS will lay off thousands of probationary workers in the middle of tax season
item.description: The IRS will lay off thousands of probationary workers in the middle of tax season, according to two sources familiar with the agency's plans, and cuts could happen as soon as next week.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NAACP lists companies that dump DEI in its tactical spending guide for Black Americans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The NAACP wants Black Americans to steer their buying power toward companies that haven’t pulled back from diversity, equity and inclusion programs under conservative pressure, and the nation’s oldest civil rights organization is listing which brands have stood by — or reversed — p
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wreckage of missing plane found in Florida woods
item.description: A plane that had gone missing Friday night was found early Saturday morning in a remote, wooded area in north Florida.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KRON4

item.title: Trump Media says it lost more than $400 million last year
item.description: The parent company of President Donald Trump’s social networking site Truth Social says it lost $400.9 million last year and its annual revenue declined 12% to $3.6 million.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AP reporter and photographer barred from Air Force One over 'Gulf of Mexico' terminology dispute
item.description: The White House barred a credentialed Associated Press reporter and photographer from boarding the presidential airplane Friday for a weekend trip with Donald Trump, saying the news agency’s stance on how to refer to the Gulf of Mexico was to blame for the exclusion.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Why you shouldn't leave your windshield wipers up during every snowstorm
item.description: Propping windshield wipers up before a snowstorm is a common routine for many car owners, but according to AAA, it's not always the best practice.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, ABC 4

item.title: Dozens of couples get hitched at snowy Colorado ski resort on Valentine's Day
item.description: LOVELAND SKI AREA, Colo. (AP) — There is nothing like dozens of couples saying “I do” simultaneously to warm the crisp, frigid air that grips the Colorado Rockies in the heart of winter.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS PEIPERT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 83 percent say president is required to follow Supreme Court rulings: Survey
item.description: Just over half, 51 percent, of Americans approve of the job the Supreme Court is doing, which is the highest amount of support it’s received in the last three years.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: Abortions to resume in Missouri after a judge blocks restrictions
item.description: Abortions are set to resume in Missouri after a judge blocked regulations that had restricted providers even after voters approved enshrining abortion rights into the state’s constitution.
item.dc:creator: SUMMER BALLENTINE and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Idle buses, empty Border Patrol boats: Arrests for illegal crossings fall in the Rio Grande Valley
item.description: MISSION, Texas (AP) — An idled Border Patrol bus sat empty this week, on standby for any migrants surrendering near the southern tip of Texas. Agents in two speedboats zipped past pockets of sandy shores, known landing spots for people entering the U.S.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4
